steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the quotation that most directly supports the claim in the text. The passage's claim is that the "overly object-focused" view is wrong because Stevens strove to convey the ephemeral and dynamic characteristics of ikaah as a cultural practice—not through the static objects themselves, but through his actions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings are best understood not as self-contained objects but as reminders of the public creations of the sandpaintings...": Shifts focus to the creation process and viewer observation—this supports ephemerality indirectly but does not address Stevens's role as gift-giver or the transmission of cultural knowledge. ✗
  • Choice B — "...they should not be confused with authentic ikaah, which cannot be extricated from a practice that is intentionally and necessarily transitory...": Contradicts the passage; the text explicitly argues against the view that static objects cannot authentically represent ikaah. ✗
  • Choice C — "...Stevens was an ambassador not only of Diné culture but of a US art culture that tended to value permanent works over ephemeral ones.": Offers an alternative explanation that contradicts the passage's framing; the text never attributes any US art-culture bias to Stevens. ✗
  • Choice D — "It is important to recall Stevens's own actions as a gift-giver—a temporary and performative role—of the sandpaintings...a hybrid that reflects the dynamism of Diné culture.": Directly supports the passage by identifying Stevens's actions (not the objects) as temporary and performative, and by showing how the transmission of both traditional ikaah knowledge and innovative chemistry reflects Diné dynamism. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
